_archetype: trend_follow_uup child: SMA(period) trend on UUP AND-confirmed by RSI(rsi_period) > rsi_min. Long ONLY when close > SMA AND RSI above its midline; flat when close < SMA. The RSI leg is a deliberate entry FILTER, not an OR widener.
_mutation: Added a SECOND entry signal combined by AND: RSI(14) > 50. Both the parent's trend signal (close > SMA(period)) and positive short-term momentum (RSI > rsi_min) must hold to BUY. Exit is unchanged from the parent (CLOSE when close < SMA) and fires alone — an open long is never harder to close than to open (close logic runs first).
_failure_mode_eliminated: Parent's empirical trades were thin/whippy: median per-trade runup +0.09% while 33% touched >=1% drawdown and median drawdown -0.33% — marginal SMA pokes with no momentum that reverse before paying. RSI>50 demands recent gains exceed recent losses, filtering out those momentum-less crosses. AND (filter) is chosen on purpose because the goal is to CUT losers, not add more entries.
_thresholds_grounded: rsi_min=50 is the standard RSI midline (gains>losses), not a tuned magic number. need = max(period, rsi_period+1) bars enforces the mandatory not-enough-bars guard for BOTH indicators. No stop/take-profit added: parent's median drawdown (-0.33%) and runup (+0.09%) are razor-thin, so the SMA exit remains the cleanest non-inert exit — this mutation changes ENTRY quality, not the exit.
_paper_only: PAPER ONLY. No real money. Mutation child; independently held to the absolute fitness gate.
_bar_limit_note: Longest lookback is SMA50; RSI14 needs 15 closes. bar_limit 100 is 2x the SMA50 lookback with cushion, and well above the ~62 daily bars the 60-90d gate windows supply.
_safety_note: safety_max_loss_pct is the HARD runaway rail enforced by runner/safety_backstop.py, not entry/exit logic.
